_comment: Act 3 / Challenge 3.1 — Descent into the Deep Tunnel (endurance combat). Loaded by beacon/sim at /spiffs/challenge_3_1.json.
_mechanic: Endurance RNG combat vs 'The Rising Water' (damage-over-time, 3 waves).
Operative must reach the beacon AND clear all waves before timing_window_ms elapses.
Session is server-tracked with expiresAt = now + timing_window_ms; expired status maps to TIMEOUT failure.
No sub-GHz or voice primitives required.
Combat is server-authoritative: the server generates and records every roll. A badge may attach optional onion.secure_random entropy; there is no badge signing.

combat._damage_note: Damage formula from engine/combat.ts: Math.floor(roll/255 * maxDmg) + 1

_no_subghz: 3.1 is pure combat; no sub-GHz handshake required (unlike 2.1, 3.4).
_no_voice: 3.1 is pure combat; no voice capture required (unlike 1.1, 2.3, 3.3).

custom.landmark: Thornton Composite Quarry area / MWRD shaft
wave_taunts: Fun fact: Chicago is nearly FLAT. 3.5 billion gallons of runoff per inch of rain. It all comes here.
TARP took 40 years to build. 109 miles, 3 reservoirs. I'm flooding all of it. Move, champ.
Still here? The water reversal helps, but COMBINED SEWER OVERFLOW is why Deep Tunnel exists. Keep running.
custom.wave_cleared_taunt: Not bad. Water's slowed for now. But there are three reservoirs. I've got valves on all of them.
custom.success_text: You made it. Here's your Sump Pump. And Fragment 1. Don't say I never taught you anything.
custom.failure_text: Water got ya. That's what happens when you don't respect the infrastructure. Try again.
custom.timeout_text: Ran out of time. TARP fills fast during storm events. Commit or drown, pal.
